zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Pashley於1435年出生在英國肯特比登登時，她的父親Sir John Pashley Sr29歲，母親Elizabeth Woodville28歲。她於大約1456年與John Stacey在英國英格蘭肯特比登登結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1474年1月10日享年39歲。

nameMeanings.name: Pashley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: nickname from Old French passe lewe ‘cross the water’, probably used for a seafarer or sailor. Compare Parslow . The name was sometimes treated like a Norman habitational name by adding the preposition de, although no such placename has been identified in Normandy or neighboring regions.

habitational name from Pashley in the parish of Ticehurst, Sussex. The placename is from Old English Pæcces + lēah ‘Pæcca's wood or clearing’. A district of Eastbourne, Sussex, bearing this name derives it from the surname; a family called Pashley had moved there from Ticehurst by the later part of the 13th century.

variants.name: Parslow
meanings: English: Anglicized form of the Old French nickname passe lewe ‘cross the water’; see Pashley .

variants.name: Pasley
meanings: Scottish: variant of Paisley . Black suggests also that some examples of Pasley and Paisley may be derived from a place known as Pasley or Howpasley, in the Borders region.
Possibly an altered form of German Pasler, a variant of Basler , or of Pässler, an occupational name, from an agent derivative of basteln ‘to do handicraft’.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Pashley was born in 1435, in Biddenden, Kent, England, her father, Sir John Pashley Sr, was 29 and her mother, Elizabeth Woodville, was 28. She married John Stacey about 1456, in Biddenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 10 January 1474, at the age of 39.

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Pashley(이)가 1435년 영국 켄트 비데든에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Pashley Sr(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Woodville(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 대략 1456년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 비데든에서 John Stacey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1474년 1월 10일 39세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Pashley родилась в 1435 г. в местности бидденден, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Pashley Sr было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Woodville, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Stacey около 1456 г. в местности бидденден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла 10 января 1474 г. в возрасте 39 лет.
